description: Landing page loads and displays product information for GymAccess
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Step 2: Verify page loads without errors
Step 3: Verify product name 'GymAccess' is displayed
Step 4: Verify a call-to-action for gym owners is present

description: Admin can register a new gym with required fields
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/admin/register
Step 2: Fill in gym name 'Nordfjord Gym'
Step 3: Fill in location 'Nordfjordeid, Norway'
Step 4: Select access type 'Shared PIN'
Step 5: Enter membership price '299'
Step 6: Select billing frequency 'monthly'
Step 7: Submit form
Step 8: Verify success message or redirect to admin dashboard
Step 9: Verify gym appears in the system
Step 10: Verify all entered fields are persisted correctly

description: Admin can register a gym with yearly billing frequency
steps: Step 1: Navigate to gym registration page
Step 2: Fill in required gym details
Step 3: Select billing frequency 'yearly'
Step 4: Submit form
Step 5: Verify gym is created with yearly billing interval

description: Admin registration validates required fields
steps: Step 1: Navigate to gym registration page
Step 2: Submit form without filling any fields
Step 3: Verify validation errors appear for gym name
Step 4: Verify validation errors appear for location
Step 5: Verify validation errors appear for membership price
Step 6: Fill in only gym name and submit
Step 7: Verify remaining validation errors still show

description: Admin can select 'Individual PIN' as access type during gym setup
steps: Step 1: Navigate to gym registration page
Step 2: Fill in required gym details
Step 3: Select access type 'Individual PIN'
Step 4: Submit form
Step 5: Verify gym is created with Individual PIN access type

description: Admin can select 'Smart Lock' as access type during gym setup
steps: Step 1: Navigate to gym registration page
Step 2: Fill in required gym details
Step 3: Select access type 'Smart Lock'
Step 4: Submit form
Step 5: Verify gym is created with Smart Lock access type

description: Public signup page loads for a specific gym
steps: Step 1: Create a gym via API or admin UI
Step 2: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/join/{gym-id}
Step 3: Verify the signup page loads
Step 4: Verify the gym name is displayed on the page
Step 5: Verify membership price is displayed
Step 6: Verify a signup form is present

description: Member can complete signup flow with name, email, and phone
steps: Step 1: Navigate to public signup page for a gym
Step 2: Enter name 'Test Member'
Step 3: Enter email 'test@example.com'
Step 4: Enter phone '+4712345678'
Step 5: Accept terms and conditions
Step 6: Click proceed to payment
Step 7: Verify redirect to payment page or Stripe checkout
Step 8: Verify member record is created in the system with status 'Pending'

description: Signup page requires terms acceptance before proceeding
steps: Step 1: Navigate to public signup page for a gym
Step 2: Fill in name, email, and phone
Step 3: Do NOT accept terms
Step 4: Try to proceed to payment
Step 5: Verify an error message about terms acceptance is shown

description: Signup page validates email format
steps: Step 1: Navigate to public signup page
Step 2: Enter invalid email 'notanemail'
Step 3: Try to submit
Step 4: Verify email validation error is shown

description: Signup page validates required fields
steps: Step 1: Navigate to public signup page
Step 2: Submit form without any data
Step 3: Verify validation error for name
Step 4: Verify validation error for email
Step 5: Verify validation error for terms acceptance

description: Stripe checkout session is created when member proceeds to payment
steps: Step 1: Navigate to public signup page
Step 2: Complete signup form with valid data
Step 3: Accept terms and proceed
Step 4: Verify Stripe checkout session is created via API
Step 5: Verify the checkout session has correct price amount
Step 6: Verify the checkout session has correct billing interval

description: Successful Stripe payment activates membership via webhook
steps: Step 1: Create a member through signup flow
Step 2: Simulate Stripe checkout.session.completed webhook
Step 3: Verify member status changes from 'Pending' to 'Active'
Step 4: Verify subscription record is created
Step 5: Verify subscription status is 'active'
Step 6: Verify access code is generated for the member
Step 7: Verify welcome notification is triggered

description: Stripe webhook endpoint accepts and verifies webhook signatures
steps: Step 1: Send POST to http://localhost:8080/webhooks/stripe with valid signature
Step 2: Verify 200 response
Step 3: Send POST with invalid signature
Step 4: Verify 400 or 401 response

description: Payment failure webhook transitions member to PastDue status
steps: Step 1: Create an active member with subscription
Step 2: Simulate invoice.payment_failed webhook from Stripe
Step 3: Verify member status changes to 'PastDue'
Step 4: Verify payment failure notification is triggered

description: Subscription cancellation webhook transitions member to Cancelled
steps: Step 1: Create an active member with subscription
Step 2: Simulate customer.subscription.deleted webhook
Step 3: Verify member status changes to 'Cancelled'
Step 4: Verify access code is revoked
Step 5: Verify cancellation notification is triggered

description: Subscription renewal webhook keeps member Active
steps: Step 1: Create an active member with subscription
Step 2: Simulate invoice.payment_succeeded webhook
Step 3: Verify member status remains 'Active'
Step 4: Verify payment receipt notification is triggered

description: PastDue member regains Active status on successful payment retry
steps: Step 1: Create a member with PastDue status
Step 2: Simulate invoice.payment_succeeded webhook
Step 3: Verify member status changes back to 'Active'
Step 4: Verify access code is regenerated or reactivated

description: Member status transitions follow valid state machine (Pending->Active->PastDue->Cancelled/Expired)
steps: Step 1: Create member, verify status is 'Pending'
Step 2: Activate via webhook, verify status is 'Active'
Step 3: Fail payment via webhook, verify status is 'PastDue'
Step 4: Cancel subscription via webhook, verify status is 'Cancelled'
Step 5: Verify that invalid transitions are rejected (e.g., Pending->Cancelled directly)

description: Shared PIN access code is assigned to all active members of a gym
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with 'Shared PIN' access type
Step 2: Set gym PIN to '4821'
Step 3: Create and activate member 1
Step 4: Verify member 1 receives PIN '4821'
Step 5: Create and activate member 2
Step 6: Verify member 2 receives same PIN '4821'
Step 7: Verify both members see the same code

description: Individual PIN is generated uniquely per member
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with 'Individual PIN' access type
Step 2: Create and activate member 1
Step 3: Verify member 1 receives a unique PIN
Step 4: Create and activate member 2
Step 5: Verify member 2 receives a different PIN than member 1
Step 6: Verify both PINs are of the configured length

description: Access code is generated automatically when membership becomes active
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with Individual PIN access type
Step 2: Create a member (status Pending, no access code)
Step 3: Activate membership via payment webhook
Step 4: Verify access code record is created
Step 5: Verify access code has valid_from set to now
Step 6: Verify access code is stored in the database

description: Access code is revoked when membership is cancelled
steps: Step 1: Create an active member with an access code
Step 2: Cancel the membership via webhook
Step 3: Verify the access code valid_to is set
Step 4: Verify the member no longer has a valid access code

description: Admin can rotate the shared PIN for a gym
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Navigate to Access section of admin dashboard
Step 3: Verify current shared PIN is displayed
Step 4: Click 'Rotate PIN' button
Step 5: Verify new PIN is generated and displayed
Step 6: Verify old PIN is no longer valid
Step 7: Verify all active members are notified of new PIN

description: Access codes respect configured ACCESS_CODE_LENGTH
steps: Step 1: Set ACCESS_CODE_LENGTH to 6 in environment
Step 2: Create a gym with Individual PIN
Step 3: Create and activate a member
Step 4: Verify generated PIN has exactly 6 digits

description: Welcome email is sent when membership is created and activated
steps: Step 1: Create and activate a new member
Step 2: Verify welcome email notification is queued/sent
Step 3: Verify email contains gym name
Step 4: Verify email contains access code
Step 5: Verify email is sent to the member's email address

description: Payment receipt notification is sent on successful payment
steps: Step 1: Trigger a successful payment webhook for an active member
Step 2: Verify receipt notification is queued/sent
Step 3: Verify notification contains payment amount

description: Payment failure reminder is sent when payment fails
steps: Step 1: Trigger a payment failure webhook
Step 2: Verify payment reminder notification is queued/sent
Step 3: Verify notification mentions payment issue

description: Cancellation notification is sent when membership is cancelled
steps: Step 1: Cancel an active membership
Step 2: Verify cancellation notification is queued/sent
Step 3: Verify notification mentions access expiration

description: Access code notification is sent when code is generated
steps: Step 1: Generate an access code for a member
Step 2: Verify access code notification is queued/sent
Step 3: Verify notification contains the access code

description: Admin dashboard loads and shows overview for logged-in admin
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin user
Step 2: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/admin
Step 3: Verify dashboard loads without errors
Step 4: Verify navigation shows Members, Access, Payments, Settings sections

description: Admin Members section lists all gym members with correct info
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with 3 members in various states
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to Members section
Step 4: Verify all 3 members are listed
Step 5: Verify each member shows name
Step 6: Verify each member shows status (Active/Pending/etc)
Step 7: Verify each member shows last payment date
Step 8: Verify each member shows access code
Step 9: Verify members can be sorted or filtered by status
Step 10: Verify the total member count is displayed

description: Admin can suspend a member from the members list
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Navigate to Members section
Step 3: Find an active member
Step 4: Click 'Suspend' action
Step 5: Confirm the suspension
Step 6: Verify member status changes
Step 7: Verify member's access code is revoked

description: Admin can cancel a member's membership
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Navigate to Members section
Step 3: Find an active member
Step 4: Click 'Cancel membership' action
Step 5: Confirm cancellation
Step 6: Verify member status changes to Cancelled
Step 7: Verify Stripe subscription is cancelled

description: Admin can resend access info to a member
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Navigate to Members section
Step 3: Find an active member
Step 4: Click 'Resend access info' action
Step 5: Verify notification is sent to the member with access code

description: Admin Access section shows current gym PIN and individual codes
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with Shared PIN access and set PIN
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to Access section
Step 4: Verify current gym PIN is displayed
Step 5: Verify option to rotate PIN is available

description: Admin Access section shows individual codes for Individual PIN gyms
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with Individual PIN access
Step 2: Create 3 active members
Step 3: Log in as admin
Step 4: Navigate to Access section
Step 5: Verify individual codes are listed per member
Step 6: Verify each code shows the member name and PIN

description: Admin Payments section shows monthly revenue
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with several active paying members
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to Payments section
Step 4: Verify monthly revenue figure is displayed
Step 5: Verify the amount matches expected total from active subscriptions

description: Admin Payments section shows active subscription count
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with mix of active and cancelled members
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to Payments section
Step 4: Verify active subscription count is correct

description: Admin Payments section shows failed payments
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with members, some with failed payments
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to Payments section
Step 4: Verify failed payment count or list is shown

description: Admin Settings allows changing membership price
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Navigate to Settings section
Step 3: Change membership price from 299 to 399
Step 4: Save settings
Step 5: Verify new price is persisted
Step 6: Verify public signup page shows updated price

description: Admin Settings allows changing billing interval
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Navigate to Settings
Step 3: Change billing interval from monthly to yearly
Step 4: Save settings
Step 5: Verify new interval is persisted

description: Admin Settings allows changing access type
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Navigate to Settings
Step 3: Change access type from Shared PIN to Individual PIN
Step 4: Save settings
Step 5: Verify access type is persisted

description: Admin Settings allows editing notification templates
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Navigate to Settings
Step 3: Find notification templates section
Step 4: Edit welcome email template
Step 5: Save changes
Step 6: Verify template changes are persisted

description: Admin login requires valid credentials
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin login page
Step 2: Enter invalid credentials
Step 3: Submit login form
Step 4: Verify error message is shown
Step 5: Verify user is not redirected to dashboard

description: Admin login with valid credentials grants access to dashboard
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin login page
Step 2: Enter valid admin email and password
Step 3: Submit login form
Step 4: Verify redirect to admin dashboard
Step 5: Verify admin name or email is shown in the UI

description: Admin logout clears session and redirects to login
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Click logout button
Step 3: Verify redirect to login page
Step 4: Verify accessing /admin redirects to login

description: Unauthenticated users cannot access admin dashboard
steps: Step 1: Clear all cookies/session
Step 2: Navigate to http://localhost:3000/admin
Step 3: Verify redirect to login page

description: API POST /members creates a new member record
steps: Step 1: Send POST to /members with name, email, phone, gym_id
Step 2: Verify 201 response
Step 3: Verify response contains member_id
Step 4: Verify member status is 'Pending'
Step 5: Verify member is associated with correct gym

description: API POST /members validates required fields
steps: Step 1: Send POST to /members with missing name
Step 2: Verify 400 response with validation error
Step 3: Send POST with invalid email format
Step 4: Verify 400 response with validation error

description: API POST /subscriptions/activate activates a membership
steps: Step 1: Create a pending member via API
Step 2: Send POST to /subscriptions/activate with member_id and subscription details
Step 3: Verify member status changes to Active
Step 4: Verify subscription record is created

description: API POST /access/generate creates an access code
steps: Step 1: Create an active member
Step 2: Send POST to /access/generate with member_id
Step 3: Verify access code is returned
Step 4: Verify code is persisted in database

description: API POST /access/revoke revokes a member's access code
steps: Step 1: Create an active member with access code
Step 2: Send POST to /access/revoke with member_id
Step 3: Verify access code is invalidated
Step 4: Verify response confirms revocation

description: Database migration creates all required tables
steps: Step 1: Run database migrations
Step 2: Verify 'gyms' table exists with correct columns
Step 3: Verify 'members' table exists with correct columns
Step 4: Verify 'subscriptions' table exists with correct columns
Step 5: Verify 'access_codes' table exists with correct columns

description: Gym entity has all required fields in database
steps: Step 1: Check gyms table schema
Step 2: Verify gym_id (primary key) exists
Step 3: Verify name, location, admin_user, membership_price, billing_interval, access_type columns exist

description: Member entity has all required fields in database
steps: Step 1: Check members table schema
Step 2: Verify member_id, gym_id, name, email, phone, status, created_at columns exist
Step 3: Verify gym_id is a foreign key to gyms table

description: Subscription entity has all required fields in database
steps: Step 1: Check subscriptions table schema
Step 2: Verify subscription_id, member_id, provider, provider_subscription_id, status, start_date, end_date columns exist
Step 3: Verify member_id is a foreign key to members table

description: AccessCode entity has all required fields in database
steps: Step 1: Check access_codes table schema
Step 2: Verify code_id, member_id, code, valid_from, valid_to, device_id columns exist
Step 3: Verify member_id is a foreign key to members table

description: Docker Compose starts all services successfully
steps: Step 1: Run docker compose up -d
Step 2: Verify frontend container is running
Step 3: Verify api container is running
Step 4: Verify worker container is running
Step 5: Verify postgres container is running
Step 6: Verify all containers stay healthy for 30 seconds

description: Frontend is accessible at http://localhost:3000
steps: Step 1: Start docker compose
Step 2: Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Step 3: Verify page loads with 200 status

description: API is accessible at http://localhost:8080
steps: Step 1: Start docker compose
Step 2: Send GET to http://localhost:8080/health
Step 3: Verify 200 response with health status

description: PostgreSQL database is accessible and persists data across restarts
steps: Step 1: Start docker compose
Step 2: Insert a record into the database
Step 3: Restart containers with docker compose restart
Step 4: Verify the record still exists

description: Database can be fully reset with docker compose down -v
steps: Step 1: Insert test data
Step 2: Run docker compose down -v
Step 3: Run docker compose up
Step 4: Verify database is empty (tables recreated via migrations)

description: Environment variables are loaded from .env file
steps: Step 1: Verify .env.example exists with documented variables
Step 2: Copy .env.example to .env
Step 3: Start docker compose
Step 4: Verify API reads DATABASE_URL from environment
Step 5: Verify API reads STRIPE_API_KEY from environment

description: Seed script creates demo gym and test members
steps: Step 1: Start docker compose
Step 2: Run ./scripts/seed
Step 3: Verify a demo gym is created
Step 4: Verify test members exist in various states
Step 5: Verify admin login works with seed credentials

description: Migration script runs database migrations
steps: Step 1: Start docker compose with empty database
Step 2: Run ./scripts/migrate
Step 3: Verify all tables are created
Step 4: Verify migrations are idempotent (run again without error)

description: Full automation flow: signup -> payment -> activation -> access code -> notification
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with Shared PIN access
Step 2: Navigate to public signup page
Step 3: Fill in member details
Step 4: Accept terms
Step 5: Proceed to payment
Step 6: Simulate successful Stripe payment webhook
Step 7: Verify member status is Active
Step 8: Verify access code is generated
Step 9: Verify welcome email is sent with access code
Step 10: Verify member appears in admin members list as Active
Step 11: Verify payment appears in admin payments section

description: Full automation flow with Individual PIN: unique code per member
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with Individual PIN access
Step 2: Sign up member 1 and activate
Step 3: Verify member 1 gets a unique PIN
Step 4: Sign up member 2 and activate
Step 5: Verify member 2 gets a different unique PIN
Step 6: Verify admin access section shows both individual PINs
Step 7: Cancel member 1's subscription
Step 8: Verify member 1's PIN is revoked
Step 9: Verify member 2's PIN is still active
Step 10: Verify notifications sent at each step

description: Full membership lifecycle: Pending -> Active -> PastDue -> Active -> Cancelled
steps: Step 1: Create member via signup (status: Pending)
Step 2: Process payment webhook (status: Active)
Step 3: Verify access code generated
Step 4: Process payment failure webhook (status: PastDue)
Step 5: Verify access code revoked or flagged
Step 6: Process successful payment retry webhook (status: Active)
Step 7: Verify access code restored
Step 8: Process cancellation webhook (status: Cancelled)
Step 9: Verify access code permanently revoked
Step 10: Verify all state transitions logged
Step 11: Verify notifications sent for each transition

description: Admin can view member details page
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Navigate to Members section
Step 3: Click on a member name
Step 4: Verify member detail page loads
Step 5: Verify name, email, phone, status, access code are displayed
Step 6: Verify subscription details are shown
Step 7: Verify action buttons (suspend, cancel, resend) are available

description: Worker service processes async tasks from queue
steps: Step 1: Verify worker container is running
Step 2: Trigger an action that queues a task (e.g., send notification)
Step 3: Verify worker picks up and processes the task
Step 4: Verify task completion is logged

description: Worker retries failed notification delivery
steps: Step 1: Configure SMTP to an unreachable server
Step 2: Trigger a notification
Step 3: Verify worker attempts delivery
Step 4: Verify worker retries after failure
Step 5: Verify retry is logged

description: Signup page shows gym location information
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with location 'Nordfjordeid, Norway'
Step 2: Navigate to the gym's signup page
Step 3: Verify location is displayed on the page

description: Signup page shows billing frequency to the member
steps: Step 1: Create a gym with monthly billing at 299 NOK
Step 2: Navigate to the gym's signup page
Step 3: Verify '299 NOK/month' or similar is displayed

description: Signup page for invalid gym ID shows error
steps: Step 1: Navigate to /join/nonexistent-gym-id
Step 2: Verify a friendly error message is shown
Step 3: Verify no crash or blank page

description: Admin can search/filter members by name
steps: Step 1: Create multiple members with different names
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to Members section
Step 4: Enter search term matching one member
Step 5: Verify only matching member(s) are shown

description: Admin can filter members by status
steps: Step 1: Create members in Active, Pending, Cancelled states
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to Members section
Step 4: Filter by 'Active' status
Step 5: Verify only active members are shown
Step 6: Filter by 'Cancelled' status
Step 7: Verify only cancelled members are shown

description: Duplicate email signup for same gym is prevented
steps: Step 1: Create a member with email test@example.com for gym 1
Step 2: Try to create another member with same email for gym 1
Step 3: Verify error about duplicate email
Step 4: Verify original member is unaffected

description: Same email can sign up for different gyms
steps: Step 1: Create gym 1 and gym 2
Step 2: Sign up with test@example.com for gym 1
Step 3: Sign up with test@example.com for gym 2
Step 4: Verify both memberships exist independently

description: API returns proper error codes for invalid requests
steps: Step 1: Send GET to non-existent endpoint
Step 2: Verify 404 response
Step 3: Send POST with invalid JSON body
Step 4: Verify 400 response
Step 5: Send request without authentication to protected endpoint
Step 6: Verify 401 response

description: API health check endpoint returns service status
steps: Step 1: Send GET to http://localhost:8080/health
Step 2: Verify 200 response
Step 3: Verify response includes database connectivity status

description: Stripe checkout creates subscription with correct price from gym settings
steps: Step 1: Create gym with price 499 NOK monthly
Step 2: Initiate member signup for this gym
Step 3: Verify Stripe checkout session has price 499
Step 4: Verify billing interval is monthly

description: Stripe checkout for yearly gym creates annual subscription
steps: Step 1: Create gym with price 2999 NOK yearly
Step 2: Initiate member signup
Step 3: Verify Stripe checkout session has yearly billing interval

description: Admin dashboard shows correct member counts by status
steps: Step 1: Create gym with 5 Active, 2 Pending, 1 Cancelled, 1 PastDue members
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to dashboard
Step 4: Verify Active count shows 5
Step 5: Verify Pending count shows 2
Step 6: Verify total member count shows 9

description: Admin can only see members for their own gym
steps: Step 1: Create gym 1 with admin 1 and 3 members
Step 2: Create gym 2 with admin 2 and 2 members
Step 3: Log in as admin 1
Step 4: Verify only gym 1's 3 members are visible
Step 5: Verify gym 2's members are not accessible

description: Expired subscription transitions member to Expired status
steps: Step 1: Create active member with subscription
Step 2: Simulate subscription end date passing
Step 3: Verify member status transitions to Expired
Step 4: Verify access code is revoked

description: HTTPS redirect works in production configuration
steps: Step 1: Verify reverse proxy configuration includes TLS settings
Step 2: Verify HTTP to HTTPS redirect is configured
Step 3: Verify TLS termination configuration exists

description: Webhook signature verification rejects tampered payloads
steps: Step 1: Send webhook with correct event but wrong signature
Step 2: Verify request is rejected with 400/401
Step 3: Verify no state changes occur from rejected webhook

description: Access codes are stored encrypted in the database
steps: Step 1: Generate an access code for a member
Step 2: Query the database directly
Step 3: Verify the stored code is encrypted/hashed, not plain text

description: Admin password is hashed in the database
steps: Step 1: Create an admin user
Step 2: Query the database for the admin record
Step 3: Verify password is hashed, not stored in plain text

description: Rate limiting protects API endpoints from abuse
steps: Step 1: Send 100 rapid requests to the same endpoint
Step 2: Verify rate limit response (429) is returned after threshold

description: Logs are accessible via docker compose logs
steps: Step 1: Start docker compose
Step 2: Run docker compose logs api
Step 3: Verify API logs are shown
Step 4: Run docker compose logs worker
Step 5: Verify worker logs are shown

description: Hot reload works for frontend code changes
steps: Step 1: Start docker compose in dev mode
Step 2: Make a text change in frontend code
Step 3: Verify the change appears in browser without restart

description: Hot reload works for API code changes
steps: Step 1: Start docker compose in dev mode
Step 2: Make a change in API code
Step 3: Verify the API reflects the change without restart

description: Backup script creates PostgreSQL database dump
steps: Step 1: Populate database with test data
Step 2: Run ./scripts/backup
Step 3: Verify backup file is created
Step 4: Verify backup file contains data

description: Multiple gyms can coexist in the system independently
steps: Step 1: Create gym 1 with Shared PIN and monthly billing
Step 2: Create gym 2 with Individual PIN and yearly billing
Step 3: Add members to each gym
Step 4: Verify members are separated by gym
Step 5: Verify access codes are independent per gym
Step 6: Verify admin 1 only sees gym 1 data
Step 7: Verify admin 2 only sees gym 2 data

description: Public signup page shows terms and conditions
steps: Step 1: Navigate to signup page
Step 2: Verify terms and conditions link or text is present
Step 3: Verify checkbox for accepting terms exists

description: Member receives access instructions after successful payment
steps: Step 1: Complete full signup and payment flow
Step 2: Verify access instructions notification is sent
Step 3: Verify instructions include the access code
Step 4: Verify instructions include gym location

description: Apple Pay / Google Pay options available through Stripe checkout
steps: Step 1: Create a gym and initiate member signup
Step 2: Verify Stripe checkout session is configured to accept card payments
Step 3: Verify payment_method_types includes card (which enables Apple/Google Pay)

description: Admin dashboard navigation works between all sections
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Click Members nav item, verify members page loads
Step 3: Click Access nav item, verify access page loads
Step 4: Click Payments nav item, verify payments page loads
Step 5: Click Settings nav item, verify settings page loads
Step 6: Verify active section is highlighted in nav

description: Gym admin registration creates an admin account
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin registration
Step 2: Enter admin name, email, and password
Step 3: Enter gym details
Step 4: Submit form
Step 5: Verify admin account is created
Step 6: Verify admin can log in with provided credentials
Step 7: Verify admin is linked to the created gym

description: Concurrent member signups do not cause PIN collisions for Individual PIN gyms
steps: Step 1: Create gym with Individual PIN
Step 2: Simultaneously create and activate 10 members
Step 3: Verify all 10 members have unique PINs
Step 4: Verify no duplicate PINs exist

description: Member cannot access admin pages
steps: Step 1: Sign up as a regular member
Step 2: Try to navigate to /admin
Step 3: Verify access is denied or redirected to login

description: API endpoints require authentication where appropriate
steps: Step 1: Call admin-only endpoint without auth token
Step 2: Verify 401 Unauthorized response
Step 3: Call public endpoint (signup) without auth
Step 4: Verify public endpoint works without auth

description: Stripe subscription ID is stored in subscription record
steps: Step 1: Process Stripe checkout webhook
Step 2: Verify subscription record has provider set to 'stripe'
Step 3: Verify provider_subscription_id matches Stripe's subscription ID

description: Admin can view subscription details for a member
steps: Step 1: Create active member with subscription
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to member details
Step 4: Verify subscription status, start date, and provider are shown

description: Database handles gym deletion gracefully (cascade or prevent)
steps: Step 1: Create gym with members and subscriptions
Step 2: Attempt to delete the gym
Step 3: Verify either cascade delete works or deletion is prevented with clear error

description: Worker handles notification queue with multiple items
steps: Step 1: Queue 5 notifications simultaneously
Step 2: Verify worker processes all 5
Step 3: Verify all notifications are marked as sent

description: init.sh script sets up development environment successfully
steps: Step 1: Run ./init.sh in a clean environment
Step 2: Verify dependencies are installed
Step 3: Verify services start
Step 4: Verify application is accessible

description: Landing page has a clean, modern design appropriate for a SaaS product
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Step 2: Take screenshot
Step 3: Verify page uses consistent color scheme
Step 4: Verify typography is clean and readable
Step 5: Verify layout has proper spacing and alignment

description: Landing page is mobile-responsive
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:3000
Step 2: Resize browser to mobile width (375px)
Step 3: Verify layout adapts to mobile screen
Step 4: Verify no horizontal scrolling
Step 5: Verify text is readable without zooming
Step 6: Verify navigation is accessible on mobile

description: Signup page has a clean, trustworthy design
steps: Step 1: Navigate to signup page
Step 2: Take screenshot
Step 3: Verify form fields are clearly labeled
Step 4: Verify price is prominently displayed
Step 5: Verify gym branding is visible
Step 6: Verify call-to-action button is prominent

description: Signup page is mobile-responsive
steps: Step 1: Navigate to signup page on mobile viewport
Step 2: Verify form is fully usable on mobile
Step 3: Verify input fields are appropriately sized for touch
Step 4: Verify submit button is easily tappable

description: Admin dashboard has a professional, clean layout
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Take screenshot of dashboard
Step 3: Verify sidebar or top navigation is present
Step 4: Verify consistent spacing and alignment
Step 5: Verify color scheme is professional
Step 6: Verify data is presented in clean tables/cards

description: Admin dashboard is mobile-responsive
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin on mobile viewport
Step 2: Verify navigation collapses to hamburger menu or similar
Step 3: Verify tables are scrollable or adapt to mobile
Step 4: Verify action buttons are accessible on mobile
Step 5: Verify no content overflow

description: Admin Members table is well-formatted and readable
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin Members section with multiple members
Step 2: Verify table has clear column headers
Step 3: Verify rows are properly aligned
Step 4: Verify status badges use color coding (green=Active, yellow=Pending, red=Cancelled)
Step 5: Verify table has appropriate row spacing

description: Admin login page has professional design
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin login page
Step 2: Verify centered login form
Step 3: Verify brand/logo is present
Step 4: Verify form fields are properly styled
Step 5: Verify login button has appropriate styling

description: Form validation errors are clearly visible and styled
steps: Step 1: Navigate to signup form
Step 2: Submit empty form
Step 3: Verify error messages are red or otherwise visually distinct
Step 4: Verify errors appear near the relevant field
Step 5: Verify error state on input fields (red border or similar)

description: Buttons have consistent styling throughout the application
steps: Step 1: Navigate through signup, login, and admin pages
Step 2: Verify primary buttons share the same style
Step 3: Verify destructive actions (cancel, suspend) use warning colors
Step 4: Verify buttons have hover states
Step 5: Verify buttons have appropriate padding and sizing

description: Loading states are shown during async operations
steps: Step 1: Submit signup form
Step 2: Verify loading indicator appears during submission
Step 3: Navigate to admin dashboard
Step 4: Verify loading state while data is being fetched

description: Success and error notifications have distinct styling
steps: Step 1: Perform a successful action (e.g., save settings)
Step 2: Verify success notification is green or uses a checkmark
Step 3: Trigger an error (e.g., invalid form submission)
Step 4: Verify error notification is red or uses a warning icon

description: Admin settings page has well-organized form layout
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin Settings
Step 2: Verify settings are grouped logically
Step 3: Verify labels are clear
Step 4: Verify save button is prominently placed
Step 5: Verify adequate spacing between form groups

description: Admin Payments section has clear data visualization
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin Payments section
Step 2: Verify revenue amount is prominently displayed
Step 3: Verify subscription count is easy to read
Step 4: Verify failed payments are highlighted or use warning styling

description: Typography hierarchy is consistent across all pages
steps: Step 1: Navigate through landing, signup, login, and admin pages
Step 2: Verify headings use consistent font sizes
Step 3: Verify body text uses consistent font size
Step 4: Verify font family is consistent
Step 5: Verify line heights provide good readability

description: Icons and visual elements are consistent and professional
steps: Step 1: Navigate through the application
Step 2: Verify icons use a consistent icon set
Step 3: Verify icons are appropriately sized
Step 4: Verify icons have consistent styling (stroke width, color)

description: Empty states have helpful messaging
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin for a gym with no members
Step 2: Navigate to Members section
Step 3: Verify empty state message is shown (e.g., 'No members yet')
Step 4: Verify a call-to-action is present (e.g., 'Share your signup link')
Step 5: Navigate to Payments section
Step 6: Verify empty state for payments

description: Navigation highlights the current active section
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin
Step 2: Click Members nav item
Step 3: Verify Members is visually highlighted as active
Step 4: Click Payments nav item
Step 5: Verify Payments is now highlighted and Members is not

description: Signup page shows membership price with proper currency formatting
steps: Step 1: Create gym with price 299 NOK
Step 2: Navigate to signup page
Step 3: Verify price is displayed with currency symbol
Step 4: Verify billing frequency is indicated (e.g., '299 kr/month')

description: Modal/confirmation dialogs have consistent styling
steps: Step 1: Trigger a confirmation dialog (e.g., cancel membership)
Step 2: Verify dialog has a clear title
Step 3: Verify dialog has descriptive text
Step 4: Verify confirm and cancel buttons are clearly distinguishable
Step 5: Verify dialog can be dismissed

description: Tables are responsive and usable on smaller screens
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin members list on tablet viewport
Step 2: Verify table is scrollable or columns stack
Step 3: Verify key information remains visible
Step 4: Verify action buttons are still accessible

description: Form inputs have focus states for accessibility
steps: Step 1: Navigate to signup form
Step 2: Tab through form fields
Step 3: Verify each field shows a visible focus ring or highlight
Step 4: Verify focus state is consistent across all inputs

description: Page transitions and navigation are smooth
steps: Step 1: Navigate between pages in admin dashboard
Step 2: Verify no jarring layout shifts
Step 3: Verify content loads without visible flicker
Step 4: Verify page title updates appropriately

description: Access codes are displayed in a readable, monospaced format
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin Access section
Step 2: Verify access codes use monospaced or distinct font
Step 3: Verify codes are properly spaced for readability
Step 4: Verify codes have a copy-to-clipboard button or similar utility

description: Admin dashboard header shows gym name and admin info
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin for 'Nordfjord Gym'
Step 2: Verify gym name appears in dashboard header
Step 3: Verify admin name or email is shown
Step 4: Verify logout option is accessible

description: Color scheme conveys trust and professionalism for a SaaS product
steps: Step 1: Review the overall color palette across all pages
Step 2: Verify primary color is used consistently
Step 3: Verify color contrast meets WCAG AA standards
Step 4: Verify no harsh or clashing color combinations
Step 5: Verify the palette suits a fitness/gym context

description: Footer is present on public pages with relevant links
steps: Step 1: Navigate to landing page
Step 2: Scroll to bottom
Step 3: Verify footer exists
Step 4: Verify footer has consistent styling

description: Card/panel components have consistent border radius and shadows
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin dashboard
Step 2: Verify cards/panels have consistent border radius
Step 3: Verify shadow/elevation is uniform across cards
Step 4: Verify cards have consistent padding

description: Action confirmation modals prevent accidental destructive actions
steps: Step 1: Click 'Cancel membership' for a member
Step 2: Verify confirmation modal appears before action executes
Step 3: Click cancel on modal
Step 4: Verify membership is unchanged
Step 5: Click 'Suspend member' action
Step 6: Verify confirmation modal appears

description: Date and time formatting is consistent and locale-appropriate
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin members list
Step 2: Verify dates are formatted consistently (e.g., DD/MM/YYYY or similar)
Step 3: Verify payment dates use the same format
Step 4: Verify subscription dates use the same format

description: Spacing and margins follow a consistent design system
steps: Step 1: Navigate through all pages
Step 2: Verify section padding is consistent
Step 3: Verify margins between elements follow a pattern (e.g., 8px grid)
Step 4: Verify no cramped or overly spacious layouts

description: Signup flow progress is communicated clearly to the user
steps: Step 1: Navigate to signup page
Step 2: Verify step indicators or progress are visible
Step 3: Verify current step is highlighted
Step 4: Verify user understands what comes next

description: Admin Access section clearly differentiates between access types
steps: Step 1: View Access section for a Shared PIN gym
Step 2: Verify shared PIN display is clear and prominent
Step 3: View Access section for an Individual PIN gym
Step 4: Verify individual codes table is clear and organized

description: Error pages (404, 500) have friendly styling
steps: Step 1: Navigate to a non-existent page
Step 2: Verify a styled 404 page appears (not browser default)
Step 3: Verify the error page includes navigation back to home

description: Dropdown and select inputs have consistent styling
steps: Step 1: Navigate to gym setup form
Step 2: Verify dropdown menus (billing frequency, access type) have consistent styling
Step 3: Verify selected state is clearly visible
Step 4: Verify dropdown options are readable

description: Pagination or infinite scroll works for large member lists
steps: Step 1: Create 50+ members for a gym
Step 2: Navigate to admin Members section
Step 3: Verify not all 50 members load at once (pagination or lazy load)
Step 4: Verify pagination controls are styled and functional

description: Complete gym onboarding: admin registers, configures gym, shares signup link, first member joins
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin registration page
Step 2: Enter admin name, email, and password
Step 3: Enter gym name 'Fjord Fitness'
Step 4: Enter gym location 'Bergen, Norway'
Step 5: Select access type 'Shared PIN'
Step 6: Enter membership price 349
Step 7: Select monthly billing
Step 8: Submit registration
Step 9: Verify admin is logged in to dashboard
Step 10: Navigate to Settings and verify gym details are correct
Step 11: Copy the public signup link from dashboard
Step 12: Open signup link in incognito/new browser
Step 13: Verify gym name and price are shown on signup page

description: Admin manages multiple members through their full lifecycle
steps: Step 1: Set up gym with Individual PIN access
Step 2: Sign up Member A and activate via webhook
Step 3: Sign up Member B and activate via webhook
Step 4: Sign up Member C (leave as Pending)
Step 5: Log in as admin and verify 2 Active, 1 Pending members
Step 6: Suspend Member A from admin panel
Step 7: Verify Member A status changes and PIN is revoked
Step 8: Cancel Member B from admin panel
Step 9: Verify Member B status is Cancelled and PIN is revoked
Step 10: Verify Member C is still Pending with no PIN
Step 11: Activate Member C via payment webhook
Step 12: Verify Member C now has Active status and a unique PIN

description: Payment failure and recovery flow with all notifications verified
steps: Step 1: Create gym and sign up a member
Step 2: Activate member via successful payment webhook
Step 3: Verify welcome notification sent with access code
Step 4: Simulate payment failure webhook (invoice.payment_failed)
Step 5: Verify member status changes to PastDue
Step 6: Verify payment failure notification is sent
Step 7: Verify access code is revoked or flagged
Step 8: Simulate payment retry success webhook (invoice.payment_succeeded)
Step 9: Verify member status returns to Active
Step 10: Verify new access code is generated or previous restored
Step 11: Verify payment success notification is sent
Step 12: Verify admin dashboard reflects all state changes

description: Shared PIN rotation affects all active members and sends notifications
steps: Step 1: Create gym with Shared PIN access, initial PIN 1234
Step 2: Create and activate 5 members
Step 3: Verify all 5 members have PIN 1234
Step 4: Log in as admin
Step 5: Navigate to Access section
Step 6: Verify current PIN 1234 is displayed
Step 7: Click Rotate PIN
Step 8: Verify new PIN is different from 1234
Step 9: Verify all 5 members now have the new PIN
Step 10: Verify notification was sent to all 5 members with new PIN
Step 11: Cancel one member
Step 12: Rotate PIN again
Step 13: Verify cancelled member does NOT receive notification

description: Multi-gym isolation: two gyms operate independently with separate data
steps: Step 1: Register Admin A with Gym A (Shared PIN, monthly, 299)
Step 2: Register Admin B with Gym B (Individual PIN, yearly, 2999)
Step 3: Sign up 3 members for Gym A and activate them
Step 4: Sign up 2 members for Gym B and activate them
Step 5: Log in as Admin A
Step 6: Verify only Gym A's 3 members are visible
Step 7: Verify Gym A settings show Shared PIN and 299/month
Step 8: Log out and log in as Admin B
Step 9: Verify only Gym B's 2 members are visible
Step 10: Verify Gym B settings show Individual PIN and 2999/year
Step 11: Verify Gym B members have unique individual PINs
Step 12: Verify Admin B cannot access Gym A's API endpoints

description: Full end-to-end member signup with Stripe checkout and webhook processing
steps: Step 1: Create a gym via admin registration
Step 2: Navigate to the gym's public signup page
Step 3: Verify gym name and price are displayed
Step 4: Enter member name 'Lars Hansen'
Step 5: Enter email 'lars@example.no'
Step 6: Enter phone '+4798765432'
Step 7: Accept terms and conditions
Step 8: Click proceed to payment
Step 9: Verify Stripe checkout session is created with correct amount
Step 10: Simulate checkout.session.completed webhook
Step 11: Verify member status is Active
Step 12: Verify subscription record exists with Stripe provider
Step 13: Verify access code is generated
Step 14: Verify welcome email notification contains access code

description: Admin Payments section provides accurate financial overview with multiple payment states
steps: Step 1: Create gym with monthly billing at 299 NOK
Step 2: Create and activate 5 members (5 active subscriptions)
Step 3: Fail payment for 1 member (4 active, 1 past due)
Step 4: Cancel 1 member (3 active, 1 past due, 1 cancelled)
Step 5: Log in as admin
Step 6: Navigate to Payments section
Step 7: Verify monthly revenue shows 3 * 299 = 897 NOK
Step 8: Verify active subscription count is 3
Step 9: Verify failed payment count is 1
Step 10: Verify cancelled count is tracked

description: Webhook idempotency: same webhook event processed twice does not cause duplicate actions
steps: Step 1: Create a pending member
Step 2: Send checkout.session.completed webhook
Step 3: Verify member is activated
Step 4: Send the same checkout.session.completed webhook again
Step 5: Verify member is still Active (not duplicated)
Step 6: Verify only one subscription record exists
Step 7: Verify only one access code exists
Step 8: Verify only one welcome notification was sent
Step 9: Verify API returns 200 (not error) for duplicate webhook
Step 10: Verify no duplicate database entries

description: Database seed creates a complete demo environment for testing
steps: Step 1: Run docker compose up with fresh database
Step 2: Run ./scripts/seed
Step 3: Verify at least one demo gym is created
Step 4: Verify demo admin account exists
Step 5: Verify demo members exist in Active, Pending, Cancelled states
Step 6: Verify demo subscriptions are linked to members
Step 7: Verify demo access codes exist for active members
Step 8: Log in with demo admin credentials
Step 9: Verify dashboard shows seeded data correctly
Step 10: Navigate to Members and verify demo members are listed

description: Admin member detail page shows comprehensive member information
steps: Step 1: Create gym and activate a member with full data
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to Members section
Step 4: Click on the member's name to view details
Step 5: Verify member name is displayed
Step 6: Verify email is displayed
Step 7: Verify phone number is displayed
Step 8: Verify current status (Active) is shown
Step 9: Verify access code is shown
Step 10: Verify subscription provider (Stripe) is shown
Step 11: Verify subscription start date is shown
Step 12: Verify action buttons are available (suspend, cancel, resend)

description: Individual PIN gym: access codes lifecycle through member states
steps: Step 1: Create gym with Individual PIN and ACCESS_CODE_LENGTH=4
Step 2: Create and activate Member A
Step 3: Verify Member A receives a 4-digit unique PIN
Step 4: Record Member A's PIN
Step 5: Fail Member A's payment (status -> PastDue)
Step 6: Verify Member A's PIN is revoked
Step 7: Retry payment successfully (status -> Active)
Step 8: Verify Member A receives a new or restored PIN
Step 9: Cancel Member A (status -> Cancelled)
Step 10: Verify Member A's PIN is permanently revoked
Step 11: Verify no valid access code exists for Member A

description: Admin settings update propagates to signup page and future members
steps: Step 1: Create gym with price 299, monthly billing, Shared PIN
Step 2: Verify signup page shows 299/month
Step 3: Log in as admin and navigate to Settings
Step 4: Change price to 399
Step 5: Change billing to yearly
Step 6: Save settings
Step 7: Navigate to signup page
Step 8: Verify signup page now shows 399/year
Step 9: Change access type to Individual PIN
Step 10: Save settings
Step 11: Sign up a new member and activate
Step 12: Verify new member gets an Individual PIN (not shared)

description: Notification system sends correct content for each event type
steps: Step 1: Create gym 'Nordfjord Gym' with Shared PIN
Step 2: Sign up member and activate
Step 3: Verify welcome notification contains 'Nordfjord Gym'
Step 4: Verify welcome notification contains access code
Step 5: Trigger successful payment webhook
Step 6: Verify receipt notification mentions payment amount
Step 7: Trigger payment failure webhook
Step 8: Verify reminder notification mentions payment issue
Step 9: Trigger cancellation webhook
Step 10: Verify cancellation notification mentions access expiration
Step 11: Verify all notifications are sent to correct email address

description: Admin can manage a gym with 50+ members efficiently
steps: Step 1: Create gym and seed 50 members in various states
Step 2: Log in as admin
Step 3: Navigate to Members section
Step 4: Verify pagination or lazy loading is in effect
Step 5: Search for a specific member by name
Step 6: Verify search returns correct member
Step 7: Filter by Active status
Step 8: Verify only active members shown
Step 9: Filter by PastDue status
Step 10: Verify only past-due members shown
Step 11: Navigate to Payments section
Step 12: Verify revenue calculation handles 50 members correctly

description: Complete signup page visual design review on desktop
steps: Step 1: Navigate to signup page at desktop resolution (1440x900)
Step 2: Verify gym name is prominently displayed at top
Step 3: Verify price is clearly shown with currency
Step 4: Verify form fields have clear labels and placeholders
Step 5: Verify terms checkbox is properly styled
Step 6: Verify primary CTA button stands out
Step 7: Verify page has visual hierarchy (headings, body, labels)
Step 8: Verify whitespace is used effectively
Step 9: Verify color scheme inspires trust
Step 10: Verify footer has gym branding or GymAccess branding

description: Complete admin dashboard visual design review
steps: Step 1: Log in as admin with populated data
Step 2: Verify navigation sidebar/topbar has clear section labels
Step 3: Verify dashboard overview shows key metrics in cards
Step 4: Verify card design uses consistent shadows and borders
Step 5: Verify member count, revenue, and status summary are visible
Step 6: Navigate to Members and verify table styling
Step 7: Verify status badges use appropriate colors
Step 8: Navigate to Payments and verify financial data presentation
Step 9: Navigate to Settings and verify form organization
Step 10: Verify all pages maintain consistent header and footer

description: Mobile-first responsive design verification across all key pages
steps: Step 1: Set viewport to 375px width (iPhone SE)
Step 2: Load landing page and verify mobile layout
Step 3: Load signup page and verify form is usable on mobile
Step 4: Verify touch targets are at least 44px
Step 5: Load admin login page and verify mobile layout
Step 6: Log in and verify admin dashboard on mobile
Step 7: Verify navigation collapses to hamburger on mobile
Step 8: Navigate to Members list on mobile
Step 9: Verify table scrolls horizontally or adapts layout
Step 10: Verify no text overflows or breaks layout

description: Accessibility review: keyboard navigation and screen reader basics
steps: Step 1: Navigate to signup page using keyboard only (Tab/Shift+Tab)
Step 2: Verify all form fields are reachable via keyboard
Step 3: Verify focus order is logical
Step 4: Verify submit button is reachable via keyboard
Step 5: Navigate to admin login via keyboard
Step 6: Verify login form is fully keyboard accessible
Step 7: Verify all interactive elements have visible focus indicators
Step 8: Verify images have alt text where meaningful
Step 9: Verify form labels are associated with inputs
Step 10: Verify color is not the only means of conveying information

description: Admin member actions UI review with confirmation flows
steps: Step 1: Navigate to admin Members section
Step 2: Verify action buttons have clear icons/labels
Step 3: Click Suspend on a member
Step 4: Verify confirmation modal has clear warning text
Step 5: Verify modal has both Confirm and Cancel buttons with distinct styling
Step 6: Cancel the action and verify no changes occur
Step 7: Click Cancel Membership on a member
Step 8: Verify confirmation modal warns about permanent action
Step 9: Click Resend Access Info
Step 10: Verify success feedback is shown after resending

description: API authentication and authorization comprehensive test
steps: Step 1: Call GET /admin/members without auth header
Step 2: Verify 401 response
Step 3: Call GET /admin/members with invalid token
Step 4: Verify 401 response
Step 5: Log in as Admin A and get auth token
Step 6: Call GET /admin/members with Admin A token
Step 7: Verify 200 response with Admin A's gym members only
Step 8: Call GET /admin/members/{gym-b-member-id} with Admin A token
Step 9: Verify 403 or 404 (Admin A cannot see Gym B members)
Step 10: Call public endpoint POST /members (signup) without auth
Step 11: Verify public endpoint works without auth

description: Docker Compose development environment full setup and verification
steps: Step 1: Verify docker-compose.yml exists
Step 2: Verify docker-compose.dev.yml exists
Step 3: Verify .env.example exists with all required variables
Step 4: Copy .env.example to .env
Step 5: Run docker compose up
Step 6: Verify frontend is accessible at localhost:3000
Step 7: Verify API is accessible at localhost:8080
Step 8: Verify PostgreSQL is running and accepting connections
Step 9: Verify database tables are created via migrations
Step 10: Run seed script and verify demo data is loaded
Step 11: Log in with demo credentials and verify dashboard works
